channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: President Trump says he will nominate Todd Blanche to serve as attorney general
item.description: Trump said at a dinner at the White House that he plans to nominate Blanche formally, according to a video of the event posted on social media by a White House aide.
item.content:encoded:
Trump said at a dinner at the White House that he plans to nominate Blanche formally, according to a video of the event posted on social media by a White House aide.

(Image credit: Allison Robbert)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Biden-era human smuggling task force expands under Trump administration changes
item.description: The Trump administration has expanded one program launched during the Biden administration: an effort to crack down on human smuggling networks.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration has expanded one program launched during the Biden administration: an effort to crack down on human smuggling networks.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: Supreme Court's Alabama redistricting decision could encourage more chaos, experts warn
item.description: The court's repudiation of a lower court decision was only the latest case in which it has played a role in changing the congressional maps for Louisiana, Texas, Virginia, and California.
item.content:encoded:
The court's repudiation of a lower court decision was only the latest case in which it has played a role in changing the congressional maps for Louisiana, Texas, Virginia, and California.

(Image credit: Kent Nishimura)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Trump strips job protections from 8,000 federal workers
item.description: President Trump signed an executive order that puts some 8,000 high-ranking civil servants into a new category of employees who can be fired for any reason.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump signed an executive order that puts some 8,000 high-ranking civil servants into a new category of employees who can be fired for any reason.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: How a single decision made a century ago split a family in half by race
item.description: Pope Leo's Black family roots inspired journalist Susan Saulny to research her Creole great-uncle who moved to Chicago, became white and didn't return. She describes her journey to reunite her family.
item.content:encoded: Pope Leo's Black family roots inspired journalist Susan Saulny to research her Creole great-uncle who moved to Chicago, became white and didn't return. She describes her journey to reunite her family.
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: A look at the results from Tuesday's primaries, acting AG says Justice Department is scrapping controversial "anti-weaponization" fund, housing official named acting national intelligence director.
item.content:encoded: A look at the results from Tuesday's primaries, acting AG says Justice Department is scrapping controversial "anti-weaponization" fund, housing official named acting national intelligence director.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Sen. Warner warns of risks from Pulte's intelligence appointment
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Sen. Mark Warner of Virginia, the top Democrat on the Senate Intelligence committee, about mortgage chief Bill Pulte's move to acting director of national intelligence.
item.content:encoded:
NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Sen. Mark Warner of Virginia, the top Democrat on the Senate Intelligence committee, about mortgage chief Bill Pulte's move to acting director of national intelligence.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: California's primary for governor is undecided as candidates vie to be in the top two
item.description: Republicans and Democrats all compete together in the unusual primary to set the one-on-one race in November. Two Democrats and one Republican were in close contention.
item.content:encoded:
Republicans and Democrats all compete together in the unusual primary to set the one-on-one race in November. Two Democrats and one Republican were in close contention.

(Image credit: Jason Henry)
item.dc:creator: Marisa Lagos

item.title: Why one of the cities most dependent on the Colorado River now has water for sale
item.description: Once one of the most dependent on the Colorado River, San Diego now may have water to sell to states that are seeing their supplies from the shrinking river cut.
item.content:encoded:
Once one of the most dependent on the Colorado River, San Diego now may have water to sell to states that are seeing their supplies from the shrinking river cut.

(Image credit: Annika Hammerschlag)
item.dc:creator: Kirk Siegler

item.title: Iowa voters pick their nominees for competitive general elections
item.description: Democratic state Rep. Josh Turek will face Republican U.S. Rep. Ashley Hinson in the Senate race. For governor, Democrat state Auditor Rob Sand and Republican businessman Zach Lahn move on to November.
item.content:encoded:
Democratic state Rep. Josh Turek will face Republican U.S. Rep. Ashley Hinson in the Senate race. For governor, Democrat state Auditor Rob Sand and Republican businessman Zach Lahn move on to November.

(Image credit: Stephen Maturen)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler
